mushroom_profile.summary: Small long-stemmed oak associate with sticky orange cap, dull yellow pores, and fine pinkish-brown scabers.
staining: May turn a little pinkish in stem
May blue slightly at stem base
mushroom_profile.season_note: Summer and fall east of the Great Plains; Upper Michigan occurrence requires confirmation.
research_notes: MushroomExpert reports eastern North American collections but does not document an Upper Michigan collection in the cited account.
Bright cherry-red KOH on the cap is a specialist supporting test, not a beginner field shortcut.
pore_color: No blue bruising; may brown slightly

records.edibility_detail: Do not eat from this app entry. Use it for recognition and comparison only.
records.mushroom_card_note: Oak-associated eastern scaber-stalk with sticky orange cap and fine reddish-brown scabers; Upper Michigan range still on HOLD.
records.field_identification: Small, slender oak-associated bolete with a sticky orange to orange-brown cap, dull yellow pores that do not blue, and a long pale-yellowish stem with fine pale-brown to reddish-brown scabers. Flesh usually does not stain, but may turn slightly pink in the stem or blue at the base.

lookalike_risk.label: High — scaber-stalk GI/taxonomy caution

records.notes: Leccinellum quercophilum is a previous combination; the cited 2020 treatment returns it to a broadened Leccinum.

mushroom_profile.summary: Michigan-described oak associate with pitted/cracked brown cap, fine longitudinal scabers, and blue-green staining.
staining: Pores slowly dark brown, sometimes through blue
Cap margin and stem base may stain blue-green
Flesh slowly gray, sometimes through faint pink
mushroom_profile.spore_print: Not recorded in the cited species account
mushroom_profile.season_note: Summer with oaks; species described from Illinois and Michigan.
research_notes: Leccinellum quercophilum is a previous combination; MushroomExpert follows the broadened Leccinum treatment.
Do not invent a print color: the cited species account states that the spore print was not recorded.
cap_surface: Tightly wrinkled and pitted when young
stem_feature: Fine brown scabers in vague longitudinal rows
pore_color: Whitish to grayish brown when young
Yellowish brown to brown with age

records.edibility_detail: Do not eat from this app entry. Use it for recognition and comparison only.
records.mushroom_card_note: Michigan-described oak scaber-stalk with pitted/cracked cap and blue-green staining; ID-only, not a food target.
records.field_identification: Oak-associated bolete with a tightly wrinkled and pitted dull brown cap that cracks with age, whitish-gray pores aging yellow-brown, tiny brown scabers in faint longitudinal rows, and greenish-blue staining near the cap margin or stem base. Cut flesh slowly turns gray, sometimes through a faint pink stage.
